senses.definition: to bring a charge against an individual or organization in a law court

examples.x: The dissatisfied customer decided to sue the company for breach of contract.
In a personal injury case, the victim may choose to sue the responsible party for compensation.
The author opted to sue the publisher for copyright infringement.
The attorney is suing the corporation on behalf of the wronged employees.
Last year, the author successfully sued the competitor for plagiarism.

senses.definition: to officially decide and declare in a law court that someone is not guilty of a crime

examples.x: The company was accused of wrongdoing, but after a thorough investigation, they were acquitted of any illegal activities.
The jury unanimously decided to acquit the defendant due to lack of evidence.
Last month, the court acquitted the accused after a thorough trial.
The legal team was actively acquitting individuals through advanced legal strategies.
The exoneration process ultimately led to the court's decision to acquit the defendant of all charges.

senses.definition: to release someone until their trial after they gave an amount of money to the court

examples.x: After being arrested, the accused had the option to bail themselves by paying the required bail.
The family decided to bail their relative by posting the necessary bail bond.
Right now, the family is bailing the defendant out of custody.
He had to bail his brother out of jail after his arrest for a minor offense.
The lawyer worked quickly to bail the defendant, offering the court a substantial sum.

senses.definition: to give a severe punishment to someone who has committed a major crime

examples.x: The court condemned the serial killer to life imprisonment without the possibility of parole.
The judge condemned the corrupt politician to the maximum sentence allowed by law.
In some countries, certain crimes can lead to being condemned to death penalty by lethal injection.
The jury unanimously condemned the terrorist to a lengthy prison term for his role in the attack.
The court condemned the drug lord to decades behind bars for trafficking large quantities of illegal substances.

senses.definition: to announce officially that someone is guilty of a crime in a court of law

examples.x: The jury deliberated and decided to convict the defendant of murder.
The court is expected to convict the suspect based on the presented evidence.
The prosecutor worked diligently to build a strong case that would convict the accused.
The court successfully convicted individuals involved in a major embezzlement scheme.
Over the years, the legal system has occasionally convicted high-profile figures for various offenses.

senses.definition: to officially hold someone in a place, such as a jail, and not let them go

examples.x: The immigration officers have the authority to detain individuals entering the country illegally.
The police decided to detain the suspect for further questioning.
During the investigation, law enforcement has the power to detain individuals suspected of a crime.
Border security may detain individuals without proper documentation at the airport.
The store security may detain shoplifters until the arrival of law enforcement.

senses.definition: to ensure that a law or rule is followed

examples.x: The police are tasked with enforcing the laws to maintain public order.
The new principal vowed to strictly enforce the school's dress code policy.
It is important to enforce safety regulations to prevent workplace accidents.
The government passed new legislation to enforce stricter environmental protections.
Security personnel enforce the venue's rules to ensure the safety and enjoyment of all attendees.

senses.definition: to create or bring laws into effect through a formal process

examples.x: They are currently legislating new measures to address environmental concerns.
In 2005, the government legislated the ban on smoking in public places.
In the coming years, governments around the world will need to legislate laws to address emerging technologies.
The president promised to legislate reforms aimed at reducing income inequality.
The parliament is set to legislate a minimum wage increase in the next session.

senses.definition: to try to charge someone officially with a crime in a court as the lawyer of the accuser

examples.x: The lawyer was hired to prosecute the case on behalf of the victim’s family.
She was asked to prosecute the case, bringing the defendant to trial for fraud.
The prosecutor’s office chose not to prosecute, citing insufficient evidence.
The lawyer worked tirelessly to prosecute the case, hoping for a conviction.
He hired an expert to help prosecute the case, ensuring every legal angle was covered.

senses.definition: to make a statement as a witness in court saying something is true

examples.x: Witnesses are called to testify about what they saw during the accident.
The victim will testify against the accused in the upcoming trial.
Experts may be called to testify regarding their professional opinions on a case.
Last year, the victim unexpectedly testified against the accused in the trial.
The court relies on witnesses who are willing to testify truthfully for a fair trial.

senses.definition: an authorized practitioner of law who defends a person's case in a courtroom

examples.x: The advocate presented a compelling argument in defense of his client.
As an experienced advocate, she had a reputation for winning difficult cases.
The defendant was represented by a skilled advocate who specialized in criminal law.
The advocate cross-examined the witness, revealing inconsistencies in their testimony.
The judge commended the advocate for their thorough preparation and professionalism during the trial.

senses.definition: a law enforcement agency controlled by the central government that deals with crimes that involve more than one state

examples.x: The Federal Bureau of Investigation launched an investigation into the high-profile cybercrime case.
Agents from the Federal Bureau of Investigation were on the scene to gather evidence and interview witnesses.
The Federal Bureau of Investigation, often referred to as the FBI, handles cases that involve national security threats.
After months of undercover work, the Federal Bureau of Investigation successfully dismantled the drug trafficking ring.
The Federal Bureau of Investigation has a rigorous training program for its new recruits, ensuring they are well-prepared for their duties.

senses.definition: someone who works as one of the members of a police force

examples.x: The cop patrolled the neighborhood to ensure the safety of residents.
The cops apprehended the suspect after a brief chase through the city streets.
She admired her father, who had served as a cop for over twenty years.
The cops conducted a thorough search of the area for clues related to the robbery.
The cops worked together to solve the complex case and bring the perpetrator to justice.

senses.definition: (of a police officer) dressed in civilian clothes while on duty

examples.x: The detectives dressed in plain-clothes to blend in with the crowd during the undercover operation.
The plain-clothes officers quietly monitored the suspect's movements from across the street.
She was surprised when the plain-clothes officer revealed his identity and badge.
The plain-clothes team infiltrated the criminal organization to gather intelligence on their activities.

senses.definition: a pair of rings made of metal with a chain attached to them, used for putting on the wrists of prisoners

examples.x: The police officer placed the suspect's hands in handcuffs after arresting him.
He felt the cold metal of the handcuffs tighten around his wrists as he was led to the police car.
The detective carried a set of handcuffs in case they needed to restrain a dangerous suspect.
The suspect was led into the courtroom with his hands shackled in handcuffs.
She heard the distinct sound of handcuffs clicking shut as the police secured the suspect.

senses.definition: the act of going around a place at regular intervals to prevent a crime or wrongdoing from being committed

examples.x: The police officer conducted a patrol of the neighborhood to ensure the safety of residents.

senses.definition: a person in a law court who is sued by someone else or is accused of committing a crime

examples.x: The defendant pleaded not guilty to the charges of theft and fraud.
The courtroom was packed with spectators eager to hear the defendant's testimony.
The defense attorney cross-examined the witness to cast doubt on the defendant's involvement.
The judge instructed the jury to consider all the evidence presented before reaching a verdict on the defendant's case.
The defendant remained composed throughout the trial, maintaining innocence despite the prosecution's strong arguments.

senses.definition: a person whose job is hanging criminals

examples.x: The hangman carried out the execution according to the court's orders.
The condemned prisoner faced the hangman with dignity and composure.
The hangman prepared the gallows for the scheduled execution.
Historically, the role of the hangman was a solemn and controversial position in the justice system.
The hangman wore a hood to conceal their identity during the execution process.

senses.definition: a young person who has not reached adulthood yet

examples.x: The juvenile was sent to a rehabilitation center after being convicted of vandalism.
The court appointed a lawyer to represent the juvenile during the legal proceedings.
The juvenile's parents were called to discuss their child's behavior at school.
The facility provides educational programs and counseling for troubled juveniles.
The judge sentenced the juvenile to community service as part of their probation.

senses.definition: a person who acts as a judge in a law court and deals with minor offenses

examples.x: The magistrate presided over the small claims court, resolving disputes between individuals.
The local magistrate imposed a fine on the defendant for violating noise ordinances.
She was appointed as a magistrate after years of experience as a practicing attorney.
The magistrate listened attentively to both sides of the argument before making a ruling.
Magistrates play a crucial role in the judicial system, handling a wide range of cases from traffic violations to minor criminal offenses.

senses.definition: a person who operates outside the boundaries of established rules and may engage in illegal activities

examples.x: Robin Hood is often romanticized as an outlaw who stole from the rich to give to the poor.
In the 1920s, Al Capone gained notoriety as a Chicago-based outlaw involved in organized crime.

senses.definition: (law) an amount of money paid to temporarily release a person from prison until their trial

examples.x: The accused was unable to afford the bond and remained in jail until their trial date.
The bail bondsman posted the required bond on behalf of the defendant, securing their release from detention.
Failure to appear in court after being released on bond can result in forfeiture of the bond amount.
The family pooled their resources to pay the bond and secure their loved one's temporary freedom pending trial.

senses.definition: an order given by a judge or court regarding a case

examples.x: The judge issued a court order requiring the company to cease operations until further notice.
She sought a court order to enforce visitation rights with her child.
The court order mandated the eviction of the tenants for failure to pay rent.
He violated the court order by contacting the plaintiff despite the restraining order.
The court order provided clear instructions on the division of property following the divorce proceedings.

senses.definition: a complaint or claim that someone brings to a law court for settlement

examples.x: She filed a lawsuit against the company for wrongful termination.
The lawsuit alleged breach of contract and sought damages for financial losses.
The lawsuit was settled out of court with both parties agreeing to a financial settlement.
The judge dismissed the lawsuit due to lack of evidence supporting the claims.
The lawsuit dragged on for years, causing financial strain on both parties involved.

senses.definition: (law) an official gathering in a court of law, especially without the presence of the jury, to find out information about a case and listen to evidence

examples.x: The judge scheduled a hearing to review the evidence presented by both parties.
The witness testified during the preliminary hearing to provide crucial information about the case.
During the evidentiary hearing, the prosecution presented forensic evidence to support their case.
The defense attorney cross-examined the witness during the preliminary hearing.
The judge called for a competency hearing to determine if the defendant was fit to stand trial.

senses.definition: a state in which a person is kept in jail or prison, particularly while waiting to be tried

examples.x: The suspect was placed in custody pending a bail hearing.
He remained in custody until his trial date was set.
The police took the suspect into custody after the car chase ended.
She visited her brother regularly while he was in custody awaiting trial.
The inmate was released from custody after serving his sentence.

senses.definition: (law) an official written document that people sign to agree on something or accept something as true

examples.x: The parties signed a declaration of intent to formalize their agreement.
He submitted a sworn declaration to the court outlining his version of events.
The witness signed a declaration affirming the truthfulness of their testimony.
The company issued a declaration of compliance with industry standards to assure consumers of their product's safety.

senses.definition: the state of having committed an offense or crime

examples.x: He confessed his guilt to the crime during the interrogation.
The jury deliberated carefully before reaching a verdict of guilt.
The prosecutor presented compelling evidence of his guilt at trial.
Despite his denial, the overwhelming proof pointed to his guilt.
She couldn't shake the feeling of guilt after the accident, even though it wasn't her fault.

senses.definition: the state of not being guilty of a crime or offense

examples.x: He maintained his innocence throughout the investigation and trial.
The defense attorney argued passionately for the defendant's innocence.
The judge declared her innocence after reviewing all the evidence.
The documentary highlighted cases of wrongful convictions and the struggle to prove innocence.
She was released from prison after DNA evidence proved her innocence.

senses.definition: the action or process of making something legal

examples.x: The legalization of marijuana sparked debates about its potential benefits and drawbacks.
The government passed a bill for the legalization of same-sex marriage.
Advocates campaigned for the legalization of assisted suicide for terminally ill patients.
The legalization of gambling led to economic growth in the region.
The country's legalization of abortion was met with both support and opposition from various groups.

senses.definition: referring to a legal work that is done free of charge, often by a lawyer

examples.x: The lawyer took on the case pro bono to help the low-income client access legal representation.
She dedicated a portion of her time to pro bono work for nonprofit organizations.
The law firm offered pro bono services to support local community initiatives.
He received recognition for his pro bono efforts in providing legal aid to underserved populations.
Pro bono work allows legal professionals to contribute their expertise to important social causes.

senses.definition: (law) a formal statement made by someone confirming or denying their accusation

examples.x: The defendant entered a plea of not guilty to the charges.
The prosecutor offered a plea deal to resolve the case without going to trial.
She entered a plea of guilty to avoid a lengthy court proceeding.
The judge accepted his plea of no contest and scheduled a sentencing hearing.

senses.definition: a formal statement saying something is true, particularly made by a witness in court

examples.x: The witness provided compelling testimony that corroborated the victim's account.
The jury carefully considered the eyewitness testimony during deliberations.
Her testimony was crucial in establishing the defendant's guilt.
The court adjourned for the day after hearing hours of expert testimony.
The defense attorney cross-examined the witness to challenge the credibility of their testimony.

senses.definition: an official decision made by the jury in a court after the legal proceedings

examples.x: The jury reached an unanimous verdict of guilty after deliberating for several hours.
The judge read the verdict aloud in the packed courtroom.
The defense team was surprised by the unexpected verdict of not guilty.
The verdict brought closure to the long and contentious legal battle.
The media reported on the landmark verdict that set a new precedent in criminal law.

senses.definition: an order issued by a judge that authorizes the police to take specific actions

examples.x: The warrant granted the police authority to arrest the individual for the alleged crime.
He challenged the validity of the warrant, arguing that it lacked probable cause.

senses.definition: relevant to someone or something in a particular context or situation

examples.x: Please read the instructions carefully and fill out only the sections applicable to your situation.
The discount is only applicable to items purchased before the end of the month.
The safety guidelines are applicable to all employees working in hazardous environments.
The laws governing property ownership are applicable in this case.
These principles are applicable across various industries and disciplines.

senses.definition: officially or legally unacceptable

examples.x: The judge ruled the contract invalid due to discrepancies in its terms.
Her driver's license was deemed invalid because it had expired.
The defendant's argument was dismissed as invalid by the court.
The passport was declared invalid after it was reported lost.
The warranty on the product became invalid after the customer attempted to repair it themselves.

senses.definition: belonging or appropriate for a court, a judge, or the administration of justice

examples.x: Legal disputes are resolved through the judicial process, which includes hearings, trials, and appeals.
Lawyers play a crucial role in presenting arguments and evidence before the judicial authorities.

senses.definition: legally held accountable for the cost of something

examples.x: The company was found liable for the damages caused by its faulty product.
If a guest is injured on your property, you may be held liable for medical expenses.
The driver who caused the accident was deemed liable for repairs to the other vehicle.
Landlords are typically liable for repairs and maintenance of rental properties.
Businesses can be held liable for injuries sustained by customers on their premises.

senses.definition: creating and enforcing rules or regulations to control or govern a particular activity or industry

examples.x: Compliance with regulatory requirements is essential for businesses operating in the financial sector.
The government implemented new regulatory measures to protect consumers from fraudulent practices.
The regulatory approval process for new drugs involves rigorous testing to assess their safety and efficacy.
The airline industry is subject to strict regulatory oversight to ensure passenger safety.

senses.definition: working or conducted secretly under the supervision of a law enforcement agency to gather information or catch criminals

examples.x: They operated under an undercover identity to protect their true identity from the suspects.
He worked as an undercover investigator, posing as a regular customer to catch shoplifters.
The undercover journalist exposed corruption in the local government through their investigative reporting.

senses.definition: capable of causing death

examples.x: The chemical spill released a lethal gas into the atmosphere, posing a serious risk to nearby residents.
Tim's severe allergic reaction to peanuts could be lethal if not treated promptly with an epinephrine injection.
The doctor warned that the patient's cancer had progressed to a lethal stage, with limited treatment options available.

senses.definition: to officially tell people something

examples.x: The country's leaders gathered to declare their independence from colonial rule.
The government decided to declare a state of emergency in response to the natural disaster.
The president addressed the nation to formally declare war against the aggressor.
After counting the votes, the election commission declared the final results of the election.
He declared his intention to run for mayor in the upcoming election.

senses.definition: (law) restatement of other people's words by a witness in a law court, which is not counted as evidence

examples.x: The judge ruled the testimony inadmissible because it was based on hearsay.
The attorney objected to the witness's statement, arguing it was hearsay and not relevant to the case.
The prosecutor reminded the jury to disregard hearsay and focus on direct evidence.
The defense attorney cross-examined the witness to challenge the credibility of their hearsay testimony.

senses.definition: a lawyer employed by a government, whose job is to prove that a person is guilty of a crime in a law court

examples.x: The public prosecutor presented compelling evidence during the trial.
The public prosecutor called several witnesses to testify against the defendant.
The role of the public prosecutor is to represent the state in criminal cases.
The public prosecutor sought a harsher sentence for the repeat offender.
The public prosecutor's office works closely with law enforcement agencies to gather evidence for trials.

senses.definition: to inform an authority about the wrongdoings or crimes of others

examples.x: He ratted out a pair of colleagues so he could stay out of jail.
He tried to rat out at the last minute.
The news reporter ratted out the people who were involved with the scandal.
He decided to rat out his colleagues involved in the illegal activities.

senses.definition: to take someone into custody
a lawsuit concerning a problem that is shared by a group of people, which is brought to a law court on behalf of all

examples.x: The law firm filed a class action lawsuit on behalf of consumers affected by the defective product.
The court certified the case as a class action to consolidate similar claims from numerous plaintiffs.
Class action lawsuits allow individuals with similar grievances to seek justice collectively.
The judge approved a settlement agreement in the class action, providing compensation to the affected class members.
Class action litigation often involves complex legal issues and extensive discovery processes.
